audit_items.proof_trace: The `s_entered` flag (L415-418) blocks re-entry into `bid()`; all admin state-modifying functions require `ASSET_ADMIN_ROLE`/`DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` unavailable to regular bidders; and `_setAssetOut`/`_setAssetOutReceiver`/`_setFeeAggregator` call `_whenNoLiveAuctions()` (L503/534/562) which reverts for any live auction. The only unguarded path is `performUpkeep` (which omits the `s_entered` check) but requires `AUCTION_WORKER_ROLE`; even if exploited, `assetOutAmount` and `assetOut` are cached in locals before the callback (L442/L446) so no net gain accrues to the attacker, and `_onAuctionEnd` routes residual funds to the fee aggregator rather than the caller. In `GPV2CompatibleAuction`, `isValidSignature` (L125-127) additionally checks `s_entered` to block GPv2 order settlement during the bid callback window.
